questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: Two identical coils each of self-inductance $L$, are connected in series and are placed so close to each other that all the flux from one coil links with the other. The total self-inductance of the system is

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: An inductance $L$, a resistance $R$ and a battery $B$ are connected in series with a switch $S$. The voltages across L and R are $V_{\mathrm{L}}$ and $V_{\mathrm{R}}$ respectively. Just after closing the switch S ,
options: $V_{\mathrm{L}}$ will decrease while $V_{\mathrm{R}}$ will increase as time progresses.

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: A circular loop of conducting wire of radius 1 cm is cut at a point A on its circumference. It is then folded along a diameter through A such that the two semicircular loops lie in two mutuially perpendicular planes. In this region a uniform magnetic field $\vec{B}$ of magnitude 100 mT is directed perpendicular to the diameter through A and makes angles of $30^{\circ}$ and $60^{\circ}$ with the planes of the two semicircles. The magnetic field reduces at a uniform rate from 100 mT to zero in a time interval of 4.28 ms . Therefore,
options: instantaneous emf in the two loops are in the ratio $\sqrt{2}: 1$.
instantaneous emf in the two loops are in the ratio $\sqrt{3}: 1$.
the total emf between free ends at point A is 5 mV .
the total emf between free ends at point A is 1.4 mV .

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: A thin wire of length 1 m is placed perpendicular to the XY plane. If it is moved with velocity $\vec{v}=4 \hat{\imath}-\hat{\jmath} \mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$ in the region of magnetic induction $\vec{B}=\hat{\imath}+4 \hat{\jmath} \mathrm{~Wb} / \mathrm{m}^2$. The potential difference developed between the ends of the wire is

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: The physical quantity that has unit volt-second is

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: A long straight wire $A B$ of length $L(L \gg a, L \gg b)$ and resistance $R$ is connected to a time varying source of emf $\mathrm{V}(\mathrm{t})$. The variation of applied emf $\mathrm{V}(\mathrm{t})$ with time is shown in Fig. B. A circular metallic loop of radius $r=b$ is placed coplanar with the current carrying wire with its centre at a distance ' $a$ ' from the axis of the wire as shown. The induced current in the loop is
options: clockwise from 0 to $\mathrm{T} / 2$ and anticlockwise from $\mathrm{T} / 2$ to T
anticlockwise from 0 to $\mathrm{T} / 2$ and clockwise from $\mathrm{T} / 2$ to T

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: A thick hollow cylinder of height $h$ and inner and outer radii $a$ and $b(b>a)$ made up of $a$ poorly conducting material of resistivity $\rho$ lies coaxially inside a long solenoid at its middle. The radius of the solenoid is larger than b. Throughout the interior of the solenoid, a uniform time varying magnetic field $B=\beta t$ is produced parallel to solenoid axis. Here $\beta$ is a constant. In this time varying magnetic field
options: the emf induced at a certain radius $\mathrm{r}(\mathrm{a} < \mathrm{r} < \mathrm{b})$ in the hollow cylinder is $\pi r^2 \beta$
the induced current circulating in the thick hollow cylinder between radii a and b is $i=\frac{\beta h}{4 \rho}\left(b^2-a^2\right)$

the resistance offered to the circulation of current by the thick hollow cylinder is
$R=\frac{2 \pi \rho}{h \times \ln \frac{b}{a}}$
no electric field is detectable outside the solenoid.

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: A total charge $Q$ is uniformly distributed over a non-conducting ring of radius $r$. There is a time varying magnetic field perpendicular to its plane and changing at the uniform rate of $\frac{\mathrm{dB}}{\mathrm{dt}}$. The magnitude of induced tangential electric field E on ring is

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: A metal rod of mass m and length $\ell$ slides on frictionless parallel metal rails of negligible resistance, A resistance R is connected between the rails at their ends as shown in the figure. A uniform magnetic field B is directed into the plane of paper perpendicular to the plane of rails throughout the space. The rod is given an initial velocity $v_0$ (towards right). No other force acts on the rod. Then
options: the rod stops after traveling a distance $x=\frac{m v_0 R}{B^2 \ell^2}$
the total energy dissipated in resistance is $\frac{1}{4} m v_0^2$ i.e. half of the initial kinetic energy
the total charge that flows in the circuit is $q=\frac{m v_0}{B \ell}$
